sections.t: Section B — Case Studies (12) — 20M each, ~250 words

qs.q: AI welfare exclusion. As District Collector you deployed an AI tool to de-duplicate welfare rolls. It has cut leakage 30%, but a field visit reveals it is systematically excluding elderly widows without biometric-linked records; the vendor calls the error rate "within tolerance"; the state government is showcasing your district as a model. Identify the stakeholders and ethical issues; evaluate your options; state your course of action.
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Disaster warning dilemma. You are DM of a Himalayan district. IMD issues a probabilistic cloudburst warning during peak pilgrimage season. Full evacuation will destroy the season's livelihood for thousands and may prove unnecessary; not evacuating risks a Dharali-style catastrophe. The local MLA demands the yatra continue; your junior engineer reports illegal riverbed construction sheltering pilgrims. Discuss the ethical trade-offs and your decision framework.
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Whistleblowing on a flagship project. As Executive Engineer you discover falsified quality certificates in a national-highway flagship project shortly before its high-profile inauguration. Your superior advises silence — "rectification can follow the ribbon-cutting" — and hints your empanelment is due. Options, evaluation, action.
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Data privacy vs investigation. You head a district cyber cell. To crack a "digital arrest" fraud ring victimising hundreds of pensioners, your team proposes bulk access to call and payment records of thousands of citizens, exceeding the legal warrant's scope. The SP backs it as "the only way". Examine the competing values and lay out your response.
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Communal rumour on social media. A morphed video is inflaming tensions in your sub-division on the eve of a festival. Arresting the local youth who first shared it may trigger the very riot you fear; not acting emboldens organised amplifiers; a blanket internet shutdown will hurt exam-going students, gig workers and hospitals. As SDM, what will you do and why?
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Civil servant's spouse and conflict of interest. You are Secretary of a department finalising a large solar tender. Your spouse's consulting firm has, unknown to you until now, advised one bidder. Disclosure may derail a time-critical national project and your career; silence is likely to remain undetected. Identify the ethical issues and the correct sequence of actions.
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Caste-data confidentiality. As Census charge officer you find enumerators in one tehsil informally sharing caste-column data with local political workers. Exposure will invite transfer-season retribution and delay the operation; silence corrodes the exercise's public trust. Course of action?
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Freebies before elections. As Finance Secretary of a fiscally stressed state, you are directed weeks before elections to release an unbudgeted cash-transfer scheme by diverting funds earmarked for school infrastructure. The CM's office cites popular mandate; the model code has not yet kicked in. Evaluate your options.
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Subordinate harassment complaint. A woman contractual employee accuses your most efficient and well-connected tehsildar of sustained harassment. She wants action but fears losing her job; witnesses are silent; the ICC is headed by the accused's batchmate. As Collector, what do you do?
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Development vs PVTG habitat. A mining lease promising 4,000 jobs in your aspirational district overlaps a PVTG habitat with unsettled Forest Rights Act claims. The Gram Sabha's consent is being managed through inducements by the proponent. As Collector chairing the FRA committee, weigh the interests and state your approach.
qs.src: UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Final Predicted Questions

qs.q: Old friend seeking favour. Your college friend, now a struggling journalist, asks you (a PIO) to leak a draft inquiry report implicating a minister, arguing the public interest. The report may change after due process; leaking violates rules; the final version may be diluted. What do you do?

Discuss the ethical dimensions — compassion, accountability, institutional integrity — and your action.
